title: Wire a minimal operator event outbox
goal: Implement a bounded append-only outbox for high-value operator lifecycle events and connect initial Grabowski and agent producers before deciding whether a persistent Chronik or Plexer service is justified.

acceptance.assertion: The outbox accepts only the reviewed high-value event allowlist and requires event id, type, timestamp, source, provenance, redaction class and related task, receipt or runtime reference where applicable.
Producers can append atomically to a local bounded outbox without requiring a network service, and partial or malformed writes fail closed.
At least Grabowski durable-task lifecycle and one agent-session hook emit redacted schema-valid events without storing prompts, diffs, secrets or routine tool chatter.
At least one consumer such as a morning digest, a Vibe-Lab experiment input or a Chronik query reads the event stream and demonstrates value beyond Git, Bureau receipts and source logs.
Chronik or Plexer persistence remains a separate evidence-based decision; no service is created merely to satisfy this task.
